concerns.text: Task T023 instructs calculating the Bonferroni divisor dynamically as `0.05 / len(actual_pairs)`. This contradicts FR-004 and Plan Phase 3, which mandate a **global** Bonferroni correction for the **full set** of 30 hypothesis tests (3 params × 2 indices × 5 lags) regardless of data availability. No task exists to implement the fixed global divisor logic required by the spec.

Task T032 instructs to 'calculate Test set correlations' for 2018-2020. The spec (US-3) and Plan (Phase 5) define the validation report as summarizing whether **previously computed** correlations (from the full dataset) hold or exceed thresholds in the held-out period. There is no task to load the global thresholds and apply them to the test set; instead, a new calculation is proposed which violates the 'Global Statistical Thresholding' plan element.

Task T021 mandates using `scipy.stats.autocorr` for the Neff formula. FR-010 and Plan Phase 3 explicitly require the 'method of Pyper & Peterman, 1998'. While `scipy.stats.autocorr` computes a coefficient, the specific iterative or block-based approach for time series with trend/seasonality implied by the citation is not addressed. No task exists to implement the specific Pyper & Peterman algorithm or verify its equivalence to the standard autocorr function for this dataset.

Task T004 references SC-001 (20-year performance goal) and the 1998-2020 assumption. However, SC-001 defines the goal as completing the 'full 20-year lagged correlation analysis' (which includes the 1998-2020 span), while the task description conflates this with the training period (1998-2017). No task explicitly covers the performance benchmarking of the full 20-year analysis required by SC-001, only the setup of constants.

Task T025 directs storing global thresholds in `state/global_thresholds.json`. The Plan (Phase 3) and Spec (SC-003) explicitly define the artifact path as `artifacts/thresholds/global_threshold.json`. This path mismatch means the downstream task (T032) cannot find the required artifact, breaking the chain for the validation report. No task exists to write to the correct `artifacts/` path.
Task T013 references `code/services/align.py`. The Plan (Phase 2) and Spec (FR-002) place alignment logic in `code/data/align.py`. This is a stale path reference from an older structure.
Task T020 references `code/services/correlation.py`. The Plan (Phase 4) and Spec (FR-003) place correlation logic in `code/analysis/correlation.py`. This is a stale path reference.
Task T029 references `code/services/viz.py`. The Plan (Phase 5) and Spec (FR-008) place visualization logic in `code/viz/plots.py`. This is a stale path reference.
Task T031 references `code/validation/report.py`. The Plan (Phase 5) and Spec (FR-009) place report logic in `code/viz/report.py`. This is a stale path reference.
